  Nothing novel emerged in this ticket. The cuid/UUID schema-type discovery was a pre-flight catch (read schema.prisma at start of Task 6 → corrected `@db.Uuid` to plain `String?` BEFORE writing any code), not a runtime gotcha. Existing patterns covered everything:
    - [[pure-helper-extraction-pattern]] (Phase 7 #7c-2 / #8e / #10 / #11) — applied to the two helpers in this ticket

PHASE_7_TICKET_12_NOTES:
  - Why factor wiring into pure helpers + thin React composition: the spec deliberately chose option (b) client-side resolution. The two pure helpers (`extractBoundUserIds` + `selectDepartmentPresence`) make 9 RED→GREEN test cases possible in node env per [[pure-helper-extraction-pattern]] — no jsdom/RTL added to the project, no React import in the test surface. The React component (`<SpeedDialGrid>`) becomes a thin composition: call extractBoundUserIds → pass to useUserPresence → map each dept through selectDepartmentPresence. Each piece is independently testable; the React wiring itself is correct-by-construction (no logic to test that isn't already covered by the pure helpers).
  - Why `default_user_id` (not `device_binding_token`, not many-to-many): spec locked option (b) — simplest schema, reuses existing users table, additive migration, FK enforces referential integrity with ON DELETE SET NULL. `device_binding_token` already exists on Department (line 184 of schema — legacy from Phase 4 Part 3, unused) and stays unchanged per scope fence. Many-to-many `department_users` table was rejected as YAGNI; one-user-per-dept is sufficient for the current PRODUCT.md.
  - Why TEXT (not UUID) for default_user_id: caught at pre-flight read of schema.prisma BEFORE generating the migration. All 13 models use `String @id @default(cuid())` (cuid stored as TEXT/varchar in Postgres). The plan's `@db.Uuid` annotation would have generated a UUID column and the FK would have failed because users.id is TEXT, not UUID. Corrected to plain `String?` — Prisma generated `default_user_id TEXT` matching the cuid convention. This is why pre-flight reads of governance docs matter (Rule 4 read-the-9-docs is not optional).
  - Why named relation "DepartmentDefaultUser": disambiguates from any future Department→User relations (e.g. if we add `created_by_user_id` or `audit_owner_id` later). Prisma would throw an "ambiguous relation" error at validate time if a second Department→User relation were added without naming both. Named relations are cheap insurance.
  - Why `<SpeedDialButton>` unchanged: same prop shape (`presenceState: PresenceState`), same 3-state union. This ticket never emits `"in_call"` (the spec deferred that derivation), so we just don't return it from selectDepartmentPresence. The button's `disabled` logic still triggers on offline OR in_call — the third state stays available for the follow-up ticket without any button changes.
  - Why ON DELETE SET NULL (not CASCADE, not RESTRICT): deleting a user shouldn't delete their bound departments (CASCADE = data loss); deleting a user shouldn't be blocked by their bound departments (RESTRICT = admin frustration). SET NULL means the department gracefully degrades to "unconfigured" (renders as offline + disabled per Decision 5) when the bound user is removed. The admin can then re-bind via the deferred admin UI.
  - Why include unbound departments in extractBoundUserIds's output domain (returning only non-null FKs): callers ask "is the bound user of this dept online?" — for unbound depts there's no user to ask about, so they're filtered out entirely from the useUserPresence subscription. This keeps the socket subscription set minimal AND prevents passing `null` into the presence map (which would force defensive handling on every consumer).
  - Why 0 new lessons logged: framework discipline is "lessons.md captures patterns that will guide FUTURE work, not restatements of existing patterns". The cuid/UUID catch was a one-line plan correction — would just be "always read schema.prisma before generating migrations" which restates Rule 4. The other patterns ([[pure-helper-extraction-pattern]], [[parallel-socket-servers-coexistence]], [[instrumentation-edge-stub-required]]) were APPLIED in this ticket but already exist. Adding fluff lessons dilutes the signal in lessons.md (per Rule 18 priority — read 🔴 first, 🟤 second, rest by relevance).
